Title: Isolation of Artemisia capillaris membrane-bound di-prenyltransferase for phenylpropanoids and redesign of artepillin C in yeast

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: YPH499 cDNA source / DD104 engineering background
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reinforcing DMAPP supply through overexpression of truncated HMG-CoA reductase supported production of prenylated p-coumaric acid derivatives in yeast
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in engineered yeast under galactose induction
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast pathway engineering and metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as an engineering support gene to enhance mevalonate flux rather than as the primary mechanistic focus of the paper
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports prenyl-donor reinforcement in microbial engineering of prenylated phenylpropanoids
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of truncated HMG-CoA reductase was used to raise DMAPP supply in yeast. 2. This donor-pathway reinforcement was necessary to observe AcPT1-dependent prenylated product formation. 3. It functioned as an enabling engineering module rather than the central discovered gene.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: p-Coumaric acid precursor supply for drupanin and artepillin C
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supplies aromatic precursor for prenylated phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mostly medium in engineered yeast for p-coumaric acid
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributed to reconstructed p-coumaric acid biosynthesis in yeast that fed AcPT1-dependent drupanin/artepillin C production
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in engineered yeast under galactose induction
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast metabolic engineering and metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background engineering module adopted from prior work to supply p-coumaric acid; not a newly characterized gene in this study
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Large fraction of produced p-coumaric acid was secreted to medium, limiting intracellular substrate access
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supply module for microbial production of prenylated phenylpropanoids
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL was part of the heterologous pathway producing p-coumaric acid in yeast. 2. This precursor pathway enabled AcPT1-mediated synthesis of drupanin and artepillin C. 3. Substrate compartmentation, not precursor synthesis alone, limited final product yield.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: p-Coumaric acid precursor supply for drupanin and artepillin C
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supplies aromatic precursor for prenylated phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Mostly medium in engineered yeast for p-coumaric acid
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled conversion of cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid in the heterologous yeast pathway supporting AcPT1 products
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in engineered yeast under galactose induction
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast metabolic engineering and metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Support pathway gene from earlier platform, not the primary focus of functional discovery here
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Precursor-pathway component for microbial engineering of prenylated phenylpropanoids
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C4H formed part of the p-coumaric acid production module in yeast. 2. Together with PAL and CPR it supplied substrate for AcPT1. 3. Its role in this paper was supportive rather than a main experimental target.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: p-Coumaric acid precursor supply for drupanin and artepillin C
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports electron transfer to C4H in precursor pathway
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supported C4H activity in the reconstructed yeast pathway leading to p-coumaric acid for AcPT1-dependent products
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in engineered yeast under galactose induction
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast metabolic engineering and metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Auxiliary redox partner for C4H in the precursor-supply pathway; not primary discovery target
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required support component when deploying P450-based p-coumaric acid synthesis modules in yeast
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPR was included to support heterologous C4H function in yeast. 2. This enabled p-coumaric acid precursor formation for AcPT1. 3. It served as a necessary engineering accessory rather than a focal nutrient-metabolism gene in the paper.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Artemisia capillaris; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Artemisia capillaris; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UbiA superfamily membrane-bound aromatic prenyltransferase; N-terminal plastid transit peptide; multiple transmembrane domains; two Asp-rich motifs
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: p-coumaric acid prenylation branch leading to drupanin and artepillin C
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers two dimethylallyl groups stepwise from DMAPP onto p-coumaric acid, first forming drupanin and then artepillin C; can also prenylate drupanin directly to artepillin C
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive prenylated phenylpropanoid with reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, antioxidative, immunomodulatory, anti-carcinogenic, anti-obesity and anti-diabetes activities
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves; stems; flowers of Artemisia capillaris; mostly intracellular in engineered yeast while p-coumaric acid and much drupanin accumulate in medium
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key terminal determinant for artepillin C formation; bottleneck in yeast production due to limited expression/substrate accessibility
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables biosynthesis and accumulation of artepillin C from p-coumaric acid via drupanin; expression in yeast confers production of drupanin and artepillin C
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in leaves, stems, and flowers, with no statistically significant organ-specific difference; relatively higher in stems
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In A. capillaris aerial organs; recombinant expression in N. benthamiana leaves and engineered yeast enabled activity
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plastid in plant cells; microsomal membrane-associated in heterologous assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S for transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GAL1/GAL10 galactose-inducible promoters in Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Galactose induction in yeast; exogenous 1.0 mM p-coumaric acid feeding increased prenylated product yield; Mg2+ required in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq candidate screening; RT-PCR cloning; qRT-PCR organ expression; plastid localization by GFP fusion; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; microsome enzyme assays; HPLC and LC-MS/MS product identification; substrate specificity assays; kinetic analysis; yeast metabolic engineering with full-length, transit-peptide-truncated, and codon-optimized constructs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First plant phenylpropane-specific prenyltransferase identified; single polypeptide catalyzes sequential di-prenylation with strict regio-specificity; prefers DMAPP over GPP/FPP and accepts ferulic acid weakly; transit peptide removal greatly improves yeast production; codon optimization had little additional effect; drupanin appears to be released as intermediate before second prenylation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: In yeast, poor intracellular availability of p-coumaric acid and export of drupanin limit artepillin C productivity; no clear plant growth penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for microbial production and pathway engineering of artepillin C and related prenylated phenylpropanes; transit peptide truncation is recommended for yeast expression; should be combined with strengthened DMAPP supply and transport/localization engineering to improve yield
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcPT1 was isolated from Artemisia capillaris leaf RNA-seq data as a UbiA-family candidate highly expressed in aerial organs. 2. Recombinant AcPT1 localizes to plastids and catalyzes successive dimethylallylation of p-coumaric acid to drupanin and artepillin C. 3. AcPT1 is highly specific for p-hydroxyphenylpropane acids and strongly prefers DMAPP as prenyl donor. 4. Expression of AcPT1 in engineered yeast enabled de novo production of drupanin and artepillin C, especially after transit peptide removal and increased prenyl-donor supply.
